solarOrphanGroup.label: Solar (NOT a numbered NEMA arrangement)
solarOrphanGroup.note: Export-designated SAs carrying NO numbered NEMA group code. This is the orphan bucket. The headline meter P031 lives here.

findings.title: HEADLINE: VINES IRR 75HP (P031 / meter 1010427495) gets zero NEMA generation credit, billed full-retail, $62,796 true-up
meters: serviceId 4699664088 / meter 1010427495 / pump P031 (VINES IRR 75HP NEW)
findings.evidence: Billing: annualImportKwh=230,223; annualExportKwh=39,718; annualNetKwh=190,505; trueUpAmountUsd=$62,795.65; trueUpMonth=12; rate=AGC (Ag35+ High Use). Effective rate = 62,795.65 / 190,505 = $0.330/kWh. That is FULL-RETAIL ag TOU pricing (AG-C blended energy is only ~$0.16-0.21/kWh; the 2.3c NBC floor would be only 0.023*230,223 = $5,295). $0.330/kWh is ~14x the NBC floor => this meter received essentially ZERO generation credit. Inventory: solarGroup='Solar' (NOT a numbered NEMA group like 4433/4444/5219), nemType=NEMEXP (export-designated) yet it nets 190,505 kWh net IMPORT. PROOF it is an administrative linkage break, not an array failure: sibling meter P038 (1010782725) sits in the IDENTICAL 'Solar'/NEMEXP-family bucket with a LARGER 124,117 kWh net import and 0 exports, yet its true-up is $0.26 -- so the arrangement's allocated credit zeroes a same-bucket meter's energy fine; the credit exists and reaches P038 but does NOT reach P031. The 1,092kW+840kW=1,932kW arrays produce ~2.9-3.7M kWh/yr, ~15-19x this meter's 190,505 kWh load, so generation is abundant. RECOVERABLE = trueUp - NBC floor = 62,795.65 - 5,295 = $57,501 (conservative; the NBC floor of ~$5,295 cannot be offset and remains owed). Fix is administrative.
findings.recommendation: Pull the arrangement's Generation Allocation Summary / Supplemental Report and the active Form 79-1202 roster; confirm SA 4699664088 is ABSENT or at 0% allocated percent (expected). File a corrected Form 79-1202 to (re)enroll P031 as a benefiting account in the correct NEMA arrangement (the same one P038 is in), then request PG&E rebill/recalculate the 12-month true-up with the corrected allocation. No new hardware. Recovers ~$57,501 of the $62,796; ~$5,295 NBC floor remains. If instead the Allocation Summary shows the SA present but the array group read near-zero exports, pivot to repairing the offline array group and disputing the affected period.

findings.title: Off-billing-account 'Solar' orphan cohort: 5 more export-tagged pumps with no numbered NEMA group (same failure signature)
meters: acct 5089901685-6 / meter 1010127899 / pump P074 (NEMEXP, trueUp Jan)
acct 3372050929-9 / meter 1010785905 / pump P026 (NEMEXPM, trueUp Jan)
acct 6539944461-4 / meter 1010282416 / pump P033 (NEMEXPM, trueUp Dec)
acct 9597876494-2 / meter 1010025522 / pump P083 (NEMEXPM, trueUp Dec)
acct 8922820273-8 / meter 1011474007 / pump P034 (NEMEXPM, trueUp Dec)
findings.evidence: Inventory scan across all 183 meters / all accounts: these 5 SAs carry the exact orphan signature of the headline finding -- solarGroup='Solar' (no numbered NEMA group 4433/4444/4624/4939/5219) and an export designation (NEMEXP/NEMEXPM). They sit in accounts OTHER than the billing account 4699664587-8, so no import/export/true-up dollars are in the billing file (account undefined). Each is a candidate for the same enrollment-linkage break that cost P031 $57,501. Cannot quantify without their bills/Allocation Summaries. Dollar value left at 0 pending bills; do NOT add to the headline total.
findings.recommendation: Pull the latest True-Up Statement + Generation Allocation Summary for each of these 5 SAs. For any showing a high net import at ~full-retail effective $/kWh (>~$0.10/kWh) with 0% allocation, file a Form 79-1202 correction and request rebill, exactly as for P031. needsBill: account-level billing not in scope of the supplied file.

findings.title: Group-4433 benefiting meters carrying residual true-ups (partial offset, NOT zero): $20,528 across P003/P052/P028/P017/P018/P027/P062
meters: serviceId 4692494679 / meter 1010100559 / pump P003 (trueUp $7,130; import 83,134 / export 76,916 / net 6,218)
serviceId 4695719808 / meter 1010016455 / pump P052 (trueUp $5,020; import 61,297 / export 55,914 / net 5,383)
serviceId 4693142227 / meter 1010106118 / pump P028 (trueUp $3,304; import 35,576 / export 32,023 / net 3,553)
serviceId 4699141870 / meter 1010016109 / pump P017 (trueUp $1,911; import 23,909 / export 22,246 / net 1,663)
serviceId 4697755484 / meter 1010260190 / pump P027 (trueUp $2,461; net -22,906 net EXPORT)
findings.evidence: These 7 meters ARE properly enrolled (inventory nemType=NEM2AA, solarGroup=4433, contiguous=Yes mostly) and DO get allocation -- their imports and exports nearly balance (e.g. P003 import 83,134 vs export 76,916). Their true-ups sum to $20,528. Unlike P031, their effective $/kWh on net is high ONLY because net is tiny (a small positive net x retail), not because energy went uncredited. Failure modes here are (c) pro-rata under-allocation when total arrangement load > total generation and (d) NBC residue on full metered import (P003: 0.023*83,134=$1,912 NBC alone). P027 is actually a NET EXPORTER (-22,906 kWh) yet still shows a $2,461 true-up -- a TOU-timing dollar deficit (cheap midday exports vs expensive peak imports) and/or NBC, worth disputing. Recoverable here is genuinely uncertain and MUCH smaller than the headline -- a conservative ~$6,000 placeholder across the cohort, mostly from TOU-shifting irrigation and confirming allocation %, not from a single administrative fix. NOT additive to the headline with confidence.
findings.recommendation: Pull the Generation Allocation Summary for arrangement 4433: confirm each SA's allocated percent and check whether arrangement total generation >= total load (if not, no meter can be fully offset and the residuals are structural). Then (1) shift irrigation runtime into the legacy noon-6pm solar-peak window to convert peak imports into midday self-consumption, and (2) verify NBC lines. P027's net-export-but-owes case specifically merits a TOU dispute. Quantify per-meter only with trailing-12-month interval data (needsIntervalData).

findings.title: Control / non-anomaly: P038 and the credited orphans prove the array and allocation are healthy (no capital fix needed)
meters: serviceId 4699664743 / meter 1010782725 / pump P038 (import 124,117 / export 0 / net 124,117 / trueUp $0.26)
serviceId 4699664172 / meter 1010243610 / Office BIG RANCH-T2 (import 3,728 / export 1,630 / net 2,098 / trueUp $0.00)
serviceId 4698660251 / meter 1009483211 / pump P004 (import 91,267 / export 107,327 / net -16,060 / net EXPORTER, no charge)
findings.evidence: Included to forestall a misdiagnosis a smart farmer would raise ('the array must be broken'). P038 is in the SAME 'Solar'/NEMEXPM bucket as P031, with a LARGER net import (124,117 kWh) and 0 exports, yet owes $0.26 -- i.e. its energy WAS zeroed by allocated NEMA credit (Exports=0 is correct-by-design for a benefiting load meter). Office (net 2,098) owes $0.00. P004 is a net EXPORTER (-16,060 kWh). So the 1,932 kW arrays are producing and the allocation machinery works for every meter EXCEPT P031. This isolates the defect to P031's enrollment linkage and rules out a systemic array-offline / under-production story (failure mode b). No dollars to recover here -- this is the evidentiary control that makes the headline finding administrative, not capital.
findings.recommendation: No action. Use as the comparison case in the PG&E rebill request for P031: 'same arrangement, same export designation, same Exports=0 -- P038 nets to ~$0 while P031 is billed full retail, so the allocation exists and must be re-pointed to P031's SA.'

cohortUpsideNote: Plus an uncertain ~$6,000 from the 4433 partial-offset cohort (interval-data-gated) and an unquantified but likely-material opportunity across 5 off-account 'Solar' orphans (bill-gated). Headline alone, conservative and defensible: $57,501/yr.
